Test N NaN NaN NaN 15 15 15

Figure 3/Table 1 used in paper¶

In [ ]:
# Writing a latex table file
tex_path = "../results/exhibit3_pc_variants.tex"

def block_to_latex(blockA, titleA, blockB, titleB, caption, label):
    # Convert blocks to LaTeX tabular with booktabs
    cols = list(blockA.columns) if blockA.shape[1] > 0 else list(blockB.columns)
    col_fmt = "l" + "c"*len(cols)

    def df_tabular(df):
        return df.to_latex(
            escape=False,
            index=True,
            header=True,
            column_format=col_fmt,
            bold_rows=False
        )

    # We’ll manually insert panel titles using \multicolumn
    latexA = df_tabular(blockA)
    latexB = df_tabular(blockB)

    # Strip the default \begin{tabular}...\end{tabular} wrappers so we can stitch panels cleanly
    def strip_tabular(lx):
        lines = lx.splitlines()
        lines = [ln for ln in lines if not ln.startswith("\\begin{tabular}") and not ln.startswith("\\end{tabular}")]
        return "\n".join(lines)

    bodyA = strip_tabular(latexA)
    bodyB = strip_tabular(latexB)

    # Build full table
    out = []
    out.append("\\begin{table}[!htbp]")
    out.append("\\centering")
    out.append(f"\\caption{{{caption}}}")
    out.append(f"\\label{{{label}}}")
    out.append("\\small")
    out.append(f"\\begin{{tabular}}{{{col_fmt}}}")
    out.append("\\toprule")
    out.append(" & " + " & ".join(cols) + " \\\\")
    out.append("\\midrule")
    out.append(f"\\multicolumn{{{len(cols)+1}}}{{l}}{{\\textit{{{titleA}}}}} \\\\")
    out.append("\\midrule")
    out.append(bodyA)
    out.append("\\midrule")
    out.append(f"\\multicolumn{{{len(cols)+1}}}{{l}}{{\\textit{{{titleB}}}}} \\\\")
    out.append("\\midrule")
    out.append(bodyB)
    out.append("\\bottomrule")
    out.append("\\end{tabular}")
    out.append("\\end{table}")
    return "\n".join(out)

latex_table = block_to_latex(
    blockA, titleA, blockB, titleB,
    caption="Exhibit 3: Phillips curve variants (OOS 2020Q1--2023Q3)",
    label="tab:pc_variants"
)

with open(tex_path, "w") as f:
    f.write(latex_table)

print("Wrote LaTeX table to:", tex_path)
print("\n--- Preview (first ~40 lines) ---\n")
print("\n".join(latex_table.splitlines()[:40]))

Wrote LaTeX table to: ../results/exhibit3_pc_variants.tex

 & (0.318) & (0.316) & (0.343) \\

In [43]:
import os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

# -----------------------------
# Exhibit 4 (reproducible): Regime dependence + inflation target robustness
# Inputs already in notebook:
#   r1_table (Michigan cross-era)
#   r1b_table_spf (SPF cross-era)
#   r3_core_table (core target)
# -----------------------------

assert "r1_table" in globals(), "r1_table not found."
assert "r1b_table_spf" in globals(), "r1b_table_spf not found."
assert "r3_core_table" in globals(), "r3_core_table not found."

def fmt(x, nd=3):
    return f"{float(x):.{nd}f}" if pd.notna(x) else ""

def pick_row(df, window_contains, spec_contains):
    """Pick a single row by partial match on window and spec."""
    m = df["window"].astype(str).str.contains(window_contains, regex=False)
    s = df["spec"].astype(str).str.contains(spec_contains, regex=False)
    sub = df[m & s].copy()
    if len(sub) == 0:
        raise ValueError(f"No match for window_contains='{window_contains}', spec_contains='{spec_contains}'.")
    if len(sub) > 1:
        # choose the first deterministically (can tighten if needed)
        sub = sub.iloc[[0]]
    return sub.iloc[0]

# ---- choose the ONE model we use for the regime-dependence panel
# This matches your Section 3 "best" headline spec:
#   PC log(V/U) + energy (tightness)
SPEC_MICH = "PC log(V/U) + energy"          # in r1_table
SPEC_SPF  = "PC log(V/U) + energy"          # in r1b_table_spf (it has suffix [SPF1y], but substring matches)

# Windows used in your tables
WIN_08019 = "Test 2008–2019"
WIN_2023  = "Test 2020–2023Q3"

# Pull regime-dependence metrics
m_08019 = pick_row(r1_table,  "2008–2019", SPEC_MICH)
m_2023  = pick_row(r1_table,  "2020–2023Q3", SPEC_MICH)

s_08019 = pick_row(r1b_table_spf, "2008–2019", SPEC_SPF)
s_2023  = pick_row(r1b_table_spf, "2020–2023Q3", SPEC_SPF)

# Pull core-target metrics (these are already for the COVID OOS window)
# We use:
#  - Core PC: exp + log(V/U) + energy
#  - Core PC: exp + log(V/U) + import
core_energy = r3_core_table[r3_core_table["model"].str.contains(r"log\(V/U\) \+ energy", regex=True)].iloc[0]
core_import = r3_core_table[r3_core_table["model"].str.contains(r"log\(V/U\) \+ import", regex=True)].iloc[0]

# Headline reference for Panel B: use Michigan tightness+energy in 2020–2023Q3 from cross-era table
headline_ref = m_2023

# Build LaTeX table body rows
rows_panelA = [
    (r"\makecell[l]{Test 2008--2019 (train 1968--2007)\\Michigan mean}", fmt(m_08019["RMSE"]), fmt(m_08019["Bias"])),
    (r"\makecell[l]{Test 2008--2019 (train 1968--2007)\\SPF 1y}",       fmt(s_08019["RMSE"]), fmt(s_08019["Bias"])),
    (r"\makecell[l]{Test 2020--2023Q3 (train 1968--2019)\\Michigan mean}", fmt(m_2023["RMSE"]), fmt(m_2023["Bias"])),
    (r"\makecell[l]{Test 2020--2023Q3 (train 1968--2019)\\SPF 1y}",       fmt(s_2023["RMSE"]), fmt(s_2023["Bias"])),
]

rows_panelB = [
    (r"\makecell[l]{Headline PCE (y/y): $\pi_t \sim \pi_t^e + \log(V/U)_t + \pi^{energy}_t$\\OOS 2020--2023Q3}",
     fmt(headline_ref["RMSE"]), fmt(headline_ref["Bias"])),
    (r"\makecell[l]{Core PCE (y/y): $\pi_t \sim \pi_t^e + \log(V/U)_t + \pi^{energy}_t$\\OOS 2020--2023Q3}",
     fmt(core_energy["RMSE"]), fmt(core_energy["Bias"])),
    (r"\makecell[l]{Core PCE (y/y): $\pi_t \sim \pi_t^e + \log(V/U)_t + \pi^{import}_t$\\OOS 2020--2023Q3}",
     fmt(core_import["RMSE"]), fmt(core_import["Bias"])),
]

# Assemble LaTeX
latex_lines = []
latex_lines += [r"\begin{table}[!htbp]"]
latex_lines += [r"\centering"]
latex_lines += [r"\caption{Table 2: Robustness (regime dependence and inflation target)}"]
latex_lines += [r"\label{tab:robustness_regime_core}"]
latex_lines += [r"\small"]
latex_lines += [r"\begin{tabular}{lcc}"]
latex_lines += [r"\toprule"]
latex_lines += [r" & RMSE & Bias \\"]
latex_lines += [r"\midrule"]
latex_lines += [r"\multicolumn{3}{l}{\textit{Panel A: Regime dependence (same model, different expectations proxy)}} \\"]
latex_lines += [r"\midrule"]
latex_lines += [r"\multicolumn{3}{l}{\textit{Model: } $\pi_t \sim \pi_t^e + \log(V/U)_t + \pi^{energy}_t$} \\"]
latex_lines += [r"\midrule"]
for label, rmse, bias in rows_panelA:
    latex_lines += [f"{label} & {rmse} & {bias} \\\\"]

latex_lines += [r"\midrule"]
latex_lines += [r"\multicolumn{3}{l}{\textit{Panel B: Inflation target (headline vs core)}} \\"]
latex_lines += [r"\midrule"]
for label, rmse, bias in rows_panelB:
    latex_lines += [f"{label} & {rmse} & {bias} \\\\"]

latex_lines += [r"\bottomrule"]
latex_lines += [r"\end{tabular}"]
latex_lines += [r"\end{table}"]

latex_table = "\n".join(latex_lines)

# Write to results
out_path = "../results/exhibit4_robustness.tex"
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path), exist_ok=True)
with open(out_path, "w") as f:
    f.write(latex_table)

print("Wrote:", out_path)
print("\nPreview:\n")
print("\n".join(latex_lines[:35]))

Wrote: ../results/exhibit4_robustness.tex

\makecell[l]{Core PCE (y/y): $\pi_t \sim \pi_t^e + \log(V/U)_t + \pi^{energy}_t$\\OOS 2020--2023Q3} & 0.652 & 0.131 \\
\makecell[l]{Core PCE (y/y): $\pi_t \sim \pi_t^e + \log(V/U)_t + \pi^{import}_t$\\OOS 2020--2023Q3} & 0.593 & 0.193 \\

\end{tabular}

Section 4: Fiscal Extension¶

In [93]:
from src.dataset import build_dataset

data = build_dataset()
df_full  = data["full"].copy()
df_train = data["train"].copy()
df_test  = data["test"].copy()

df_full.shape, df_train.shape, df_test.shape

In [94]:
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

fiscal_path = Path("../rawdata/fiscal_fred_quarterly.csv")
fiscal = pd.read_csv(fiscal_path)

fiscal["period_q"] = pd.PeriodIndex(fiscal["period_q"], freq="Q")

# Keep the already-computed impulse measures from the CSV
need = ["period_q", "deficit_gdp", "fiscal_impulse_d4", "fiscal_impulse_d1"]
missing = [c for c in need if c not in fiscal.columns]
if missing:
    raise ValueError(f"Missing columns in fiscal CSV: {missing}")

fiscal = fiscal[need].copy()

# Merge ONLY once into df_full, then split from df_full so everything is aligned
df_full = df_full.merge(fiscal, on="period_q", how="left").sort_values("period_q")

train_end = pd.Period("2019Q4", freq="Q")
test_start = pd.Period("2020Q1", freq="Q")

df_train = df_full[df_full["period_q"] <= train_end].copy()
df_test  = df_full[df_full["period_q"] >= test_start].copy()

# Sanity check: you should now have non-missing d4 from 2020Q1 onward
df_test.loc[df_test["period_q"] <= pd.Period("2020Q4","Q"), ["period_q","deficit_gdp","fiscal_impulse_d4","fiscal_impulse_d1"]]
Out[94]:
period_q deficit_gdp fiscal_impulse_d4 fiscal_impulse_d1
208 2020Q1 0.049441 0.000385 0.002963
209 2020Q2 0.268403 0.219079 0.218962
210 2020Q3 0.155569 0.106544 -0.112834
211 2020Q4 0.089871 0.043392 -0.065698
In [95]:
import numpy as np

y = "pi_pce_yoy"
spec_base = ["exp_mich_1y_mean", "log_vu", "pi_pce_energy_yoy"]

def rmse_mae_bias(oos_out):
    e = oos_out["dtest"]["err"].astype(float)
    return float(np.sqrt(np.mean(e**2))), float(np.mean(np.abs(e))), float(np.mean(e))

# Baseline
train_base = df_train.dropna(subset=[y] + spec_base)
test_base  = df_test.dropna(subset=[y] + spec_base)
out_base = oos_eval(train_base, test_base, y, spec_base, hac_lags=4)

# Fiscal Δ4
spec_d4 = spec_base + ["fiscal_impulse_d4"]
train_d4 = df_train.dropna(subset=[y] + spec_d4)
test_d4  = df_test.dropna(subset=[y] + spec_d4)
out_d4 = oos_eval(train_d4, test_d4, y, spec_d4, hac_lags=4)

# Fiscal Δ1
spec_d1 = spec_base + ["fiscal_impulse_d1"]
train_d1 = df_train.dropna(subset=[y] + spec_d1)
test_d1  = df_test.dropna(subset=[y] + spec_d1)
out_d1 = oos_eval(train_d1, test_d1, y, spec_d1, hac_lags=4)

print("N test (base/d4/d1):", len(out_base["dtest"]), len(out_d4["dtest"]), len(out_d1["dtest"]))

rb = rmse_mae_bias(out_base)
r4 = rmse_mae_bias(out_d4)
r1 = rmse_mae_bias(out_d1)

print("Base  RMSE/MAE/Bias:", tuple(round(x,3) for x in rb))
print("Δ4    RMSE/MAE/Bias:", tuple(round(x,3) for x in r4))
print("Δ1    RMSE/MAE/Bias:", tuple(round(x,3) for x in r1))

N test (base/d4/d1): 15 15 15
Base  RMSE/MAE/Bias: (0.563, 0.456, 0.305)
Δ4    RMSE/MAE/Bias: (2.134, 1.495, 0.53)
Δ1    RMSE/MAE/Bias: (2.712, 1.626, 0.372)
In [96]:
# --- Exhibit 5: Fiscal extension (errors + fiscal impulse overlay), 2020Q1–2023Q3 ---

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# REQUIREMENTS:
# - out_base: output from oos_eval() for baseline best PC spec
# - out_d4:   output from oos_eval() for baseline + fiscal_impulse_d4
# - df_test:  test dataframe (2020Q1–2023Q3) that contains period_q/date and fiscal_impulse_d4
#
# out_base["dtest"] and out_d4["dtest"] must contain: ["period_q","date","err"] at minimum.

# 1) Collect errors and merge to common index
e_base = out_base["dtest"][["period_q", "date", "err"]].copy().rename(columns={"err": "err_base"})
e_fisc = out_d4["dtest"][["period_q", "date", "err"]].copy().rename(columns={"err": "err_fisc"})

d = (
    e_base.merge(e_fisc, on=["period_q", "date"], how="inner")
          .merge(df_test[["period_q", "date", "fiscal_impulse_d4"]], on=["period_q", "date"], how="left")
          .sort_values("date")
)

# 2) Safety checks (helpful when debugging)
assert d["err_base"].notna().all(), "Missing baseline errors after merge."
assert d["err_fisc"].notna().all(), "Missing fiscal-augmented errors after merge."
assert d["fiscal_impulse_d4"].notna().all(), "Missing fiscal_impulse_d4 values in merged data."

# 3) Plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))

ax.plot(d["date"], d["err_base"], label="Error: Best PC", linewidth=2)
ax.plot(d["date"], d["err_fisc"], label="Error: + Fiscal impulse (Δ4 deficit/GDP)", linewidth=2)

ax.axhline(0, linewidth=1)
ax.set_title("Fiscal extension: forecast errors and fiscal impulse (2020Q1–2023Q3)")
ax.set_ylabel("Forecast error (actual − forecast)")
ax.set_xlabel("")

# Secondary axis for fiscal impulse
ax2 = ax.twinx()
ax2.plot(d["date"], d["fiscal_impulse_d4"], linestyle="--", linewidth=2, label="Fiscal impulse Δ4(deficit/GDP)")
ax2.set_ylabel("Δ4(deficit/GDP)")

# Combined legend
lines1, labels1 = ax.get_legend_handles_labels()
lines2, labels2 = ax2.get_legend_handles_labels()
ax.legend(lines1 + lines2, labels1 + labels2, loc="lower left", bbox_to_anchor=(0, -0.5))

fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# 4) Optional: save
# fig.savefig("../results/figures/exhibit5_fiscal_errors_overlay.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
In [97]:
# --- Exhibit 5 (supporting text): coefficient output (train ≤2019Q4) ---
# Assumes your oos_eval() returns a statsmodels regression result object in out["res"].
# If your key is different, run: out_base.keys() and replace "res" accordingly.

import numpy as np

# Grab fitted (training) regression results
res_base = out_base["res"]   # baseline: exp + log_vu + energy
res_fisc = out_d4["res"]     # fiscal:   exp + log_vu + energy + fiscal_impulse_d4

def print_coefs(res, vars_list, title):
    print(title)
    for v in vars_list:
        b  = float(res.params[v])
        se = float(res.bse[v])
        t  = b / se if se != 0 else np.nan
        print(f"  {v:20s} {b:9.3f}  ({se:7.3f})   t={t:6.2f}")
    print()

vars_base = ["exp_mich_1y_mean", "log_vu", "pi_pce_energy_yoy"]
vars_fisc = vars_base + ["fiscal_impulse_d4"]

print_coefs(res_base, vars_base, "Baseline (train ≤2019Q4):")
print_coefs(res_fisc, vars_fisc, "+ Fiscal impulse Δ4(deficit/GDP) (train ≤2019Q4):")

Baseline (train ≤2019Q4):
  exp_mich_1y_mean         1.023  (  0.082)   t= 12.49
  log_vu                  -0.082  (  0.316)   t= -0.26
  pi_pce_energy_yoy        0.041  (  0.010)   t=  3.89

+ Fiscal impulse Δ4(deficit/GDP) (train ≤2019Q4):
  exp_mich_1y_mean         1.021  (  0.078)   t= 13.03
  log_vu                   0.159  (  0.256)   t=  0.62
  pi_pce_energy_yoy        0.043  (  0.011)   t=  3.81
  fiscal_impulse_d4       22.838  ( 11.845)   t=  1.93
